sections.text: Every time you open a website, something amazing happens in less than a second. Your computer talks to another computer far away, asks for a page, and gets it back. Let's break down exactly what happens when you use the internet.

sections.title: What is the Internet?
sections.text: The internet is a giant network of computers all connected to each other, all over the world. These computers can send messages to one another through cables, wires, and even satellites. When you go on a website, your computer is just asking another computer to send it some information.

sections.title: What is the Web?
sections.text: The web, short for World Wide Web, is one way people use the internet. It is made of pages full of text, pictures, and videos that are linked together. You click a link on one page and it takes you to another page. The internet is the network of roads, and the web is one type of car driving on it.

sections.title: What Happens When You Visit a Website
items: You type a website address into your browser and press enter.
Your browser asks a special computer called a server: can you send me this page?
The server finds the page and sends it back to your browser.
Your browser reads the page and shows it to you on the screen.
This whole trip can happen in a tiny fraction of a second, even if the server is on the other side of the world.

sections.title: Think of it like Ordering Food
sections.text: Visiting a website is like calling a restaurant and asking for a pizza. You are the browser, the restaurant is the server. You ask for what you want, the restaurant makes it, and then sends it back to you. You don't need to know how the kitchen works, you just need to ask and wait for your order.
The internet does not have one single owner. It is made up of millions of computers and networks all around the world, all agreeing to talk to each other using the same rules.

terms.definition: A huge network of computers around the world that can send messages to each other.
Short for World Wide Web, a system of linked pages that you can visit using a browser.
A computer that stores websites and sends them to your browser when you ask for them.
The app on your device, like Chrome or Safari, that asks for web pages and shows them to you.
A group of computers that are connected together and can talk to each other.

options: A single computer in one building
A huge network of computers connected around the world
questions.explanation: The internet is a giant network made of millions of computers all over the world that are connected and can send information to each other.

options: A computer that stores websites and sends them when asked
questions.explanation: A server is a computer that stores web pages and other files, and sends them to your browser whenever you ask for them.

questions.text: What does your browser do when you visit a website?
options: It builds the website from scratch by itself
It asks a server for the page and then shows it to you
questions.explanation: Your browser sends a request to a server asking for the page, then once it gets the page back, it shows it to you on your screen.
